provenance.source_corpus: Glyphosate, steam and cutting for non‐native plant control in Alberta fescue grassland restoration

source_spans.text: Glyphosate application reduced non-native grasses, but not non-native forbs.
Glyphosate reduced non-native grass cover for three growing seasons following application
Glyphosate led to significant increases in non-native forb cover
native forb cover had significantly decreased with glyphosate at Site 3
native forb cover and richness were significantly lower with glyphosate than controls at Site 3
Non-native grass species richness and diversity were significantly lower with glyphosate
Non-native grass species richness and diversity were significantly lower with glyphosate
Total diversity was significantly lower with glyphosate across sites in 2011
Flowering and seed set of non-native grasses was significantly reduced with glyphosate at Site 1
Flowering and seed set of non-native grasses was significantly reduced with glyphosate at Site 1
that of non-native forbs was significantly reduced with cutting and glyphosate at Site 2
that of non-native forbs was significantly reduced with cutting and glyphosate at Site 2

nodes.name: Native forb flowering and seed production

source_spans.text: it significantly reduced that of native grasses and forbs

nodes.name: Native grass flowering and seed production

source_spans.text: it significantly reduced that of native grasses and forbs
Glyphosate shifted communities dominated by non-native perennial grasses to dominance by non-native forbs
Glyphosate shifted communities dominated by non-native perennial grasses to dominance by non-native forbs
Cutting and steam had little significant impact on vegetation diversity and species richness
steam reduced non-native grass cover at the most heavily invaded site
The native forb species Heterotheca villosa (Pursch) Shinners (hairy false golden aster) and Achillea millefolium L. (common yarrow) were eliminated by glyphosate.
The native forb species Heterotheca villosa (Pursch) Shinners (hairy false golden aster) and Achillea millefolium L. (common yarrow) were eliminated by glyphosate.

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:33f76269a2b6
edges.original_sentence: Glyphosate reduced non-native grass cover for three growing seasons following application and non-native forbs for one growing season.

source_spans.text: Glyphosate reduced non-native grass cover for three growing seasons following application and non-native forbs for one growing season.

source_document.title: Glyphosate, steam and cutting for non‐native plant control in Alberta fescue grassland restoration

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:33f76269a2b6
edges.original_sentence: Glyphosate led to significant increases in non-native forb cover, more than double pre-existing values 2 and 3 yr after application.

source_spans.text: Glyphosate led to significant increases in non-native forb cover, more than double pre-existing values 2 and 3 yr after application.

edges.original_sentence: By the end of the study non-native forb cover was significantly higher with glyphosate than pre-treatment at Sites 1 and 2, and native forb cover had significantly decreased with glyphosate at Site 3
